claim: Known findings only; undiscovered defects remain possible.

findings.exploit: Consumers can resolve unpublished internal distributions or receive a wheel missing import packages.
findings.correction: One root distribution contains all five import packages and has no unpublished cpcf-* dependencies.

findings.exploit: A first-time agent requires an API before it can discover CPCF semantics or schemas.
findings.correction: Offline explain, self-check, schema list/show, and bundle verification are installed in the base wheel.

findings.exploit: Local paths, credentials, caches, or generated artifacts can be committed or packaged.
findings.correction: Explicit source manifest, staged/archive hygiene scanner, narrow Gitleaks allowlist, and expanded ignores.

findings.exploit: A manual workflow or mismatched tag can upload an unreviewed distribution.
findings.correction: Release-only trusted publishing enforces the exact artifact contract, version match, protected pypi environment, explicit publication variable, and recorded kadubon self-review governance downgrade. Beta package publication is distinct from the strict operational-evidence decision.

findings.exploit: Previously admitted records can be used without recomputing signatures, time, source, lifecycle, and projection authority.
findings.correction: A single loader now recomputes the ledger, DSSE, trusted time, lifecycle, source artifacts, evidence pointers, and quorum while rejecting unsigned native authority; API-wide integration remains required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_authority.py and release gate: authoritative read integration suite

findings.exploit: A crash can separate CAS references, ledger state, idempotency, outbox, audit, and current generation.
findings.correction: The PostgreSQL generation unit of work now groups ledger, generation, audit, idempotency, outbox, quarantine, and pointer changes under serializable optimistic locking; live crash-matrix evidence and API-wide use remain required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_db_worker_assurance.py::test_generation_unit_of_work_commits_all_database_effects_or_quarantines and release gate: PostgreSQL generation crash matrix

findings.exploit: A stale or replayed runner lease can submit unbound output or hide incomplete cleanup.
findings.correction: The reference gateway now validates sanitized certificate identities, independently signed capability and execution policies, leases, attempts, bounded artifacts, DSSE receipts, cleanup, and pending-only projections. A multi-replica PostgreSQL repository, deployed Envoy sidecar, and integration evidence remain required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_runner_gateway.py and release gate: deployed mTLS lease replay and artifact integrity suite

findings.exploit: Incomplete siphon, coupling, cut, service-curve, or occurrence analysis can be mistaken for a negative result.
findings.correction: Closed result kinds, an integrated intervention portfolio, inhibited generalized/generative RAF enumeration, and bounded exact reference algorithms preserve exhaustion; large-network and mutation release evidence remains required.

findings.exploit: A perturbation can reuse baseline facts after expiry, revocation, or a typed value change.
findings.correction: Typed removal selectors, trusted-time advancement, replacement binding, and fresh shared-kernel replay are implemented; loader-authenticated replacements and every revocation/value-change integration path remain required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_science_assurance.py and release gate: authoritative fresh-audit equivalence for every perturbation class

findings.exploit: An abstract planner successor can retain science, authority, trial, or independence that an outcome removed.
findings.correction: Every branch now keeps hypothetical output pending, propagates exact resource and operational facets, applies worst-case removals, and invokes the shared audit kernel; API enforcement of loader-approved capabilities remains required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_planning_assurance.py and release gate: authoritative full-snapshot AND-OR differential suite

findings.exploit: Unsigned coordination or trial records can be treated as admitted upstream state.
findings.correction: The authoritative loader binds typed actors to verified signers and requires recomputed protocol, amendment, and result quorums. Coordination and trial kernels validate complete admitted state; production worker admission integration remains required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_authority.py and tests/test_v6_coordination_trial_assurance.py; release gate: PostgreSQL coordination and trial admission suite

findings.exploit: Onboarding can present static blockers that omit or misstate live workspace state.
findings.correction: A typed aggregator now reports every required subsystem, preserves unknowns, and generates non-executable unbound repairs. Production PostgreSQL diagnostic population remains required.
findings.regression_test: tests/test_v6_onboarding_repairs.py and tests/test_v6_api_assurance.py; release gate: installed-wheel PostgreSQL onboarding conformance

findings.exploit: Passing regression counts can conceal weak branch or mutation assurance.
findings.correction: The full combined branch-enabled gate exceeds 90 percent and each named critical group is checked independently at 95 percent. Final-commit mutation, live integration, deployed load/chaos/restore, soak, threat-model, and penetration gates remain blocking.
findings.regression_test: scripts/check_critical_coverage.py, tests/test_v6_operations_release.py, .github/workflows/ci.yml, and external release evidence

findings.exploit: The v0.6 read-only legacy bridge can forward a v0.1-v0.5 mutation or local process command.
findings.correction: The bridge uses an explicit read-only command registry and rejects apply or mutation commands before legacy dispatch.

findings.exploit: Platform newline conversion, OS-specific type surfaces, invalid PostgreSQL utility binding, or an incomplete Mutmut shadow tree can make release evidence non-portable or absent.
findings.correction: Generated references force LF, platform-only APIs use runtime module dictionaries, role DDL uses a safely composed literal, and Mutmut copies the full source tree while mutating only critical modules.

findings.exploit: An online Alembic command can report a successful upgrade while its uncommitted DDL is rolled back when the connection closes.
findings.correction: The online migration now configures and executes every revision inside Alembic's explicit transaction boundary.

findings.exploit: A Mutmut results command can fail before computing the release score, leaving mutation assurance absent despite a completed mutation run.
findings.correction: CI and release workflows pass an explicit Boolean value to Mutmut 3.6 and retain the 85 percent fail-closed score check.

findings.exploit: A repository owner can approve the pypi environment and bypass independent human release review.
findings.correction: The pypi environment records kadubon with prevent_self_review=false exactly as configured, and all audit and release documents label this as self-approval rather than independent review.
findings.regression_test: GitHub environment settings audit and docs/release.md

findings.exploit: A declared branch output could enter live evidence and satisfy a blocker before a runner receipt and independent projection approval existed.
findings.correction: Branch additions create pending projections only; live evidence changes only through authoritative promotion.

findings.exploit: A perturbation caller could choose an unsigned evaluation timestamp and manufacture expiry or continued validity.
findings.correction: Perturbation time advancement now requires a scoped monotonic trusted-time receipt digest.

findings.exploit: Duplicate live typed identifiers could be silently overwritten during index construction.
findings.correction: The provenance kernel rejects duplicate live identities before scientific indexing.

findings.exploit: A finite-horizon persistence plan could use unmeasured or interval-infeasible action rates, and a zero-lower-bound supply could discharge a siphon obligation.
findings.correction: Every used transformation requires a live rate interval, exact action rate must lie inside it, and fed-siphon credit requires positive lower supply.

findings.exploit: A package release could be represented as operational assurance after only automated source checks, without sustained availability, intended-deployment restore, threat review, or penetration evidence.
findings.correction: Beta package publication is explicitly classified and reports unavailable external evidence without treating it as satisfied. The strict default operational validator still requires independent review, a 30-day soak, intended-deployment restore, load, chaos, and zero-blocker penetration evidence.

findings.exploit: API and worker images can build without their optional runtime dependencies and then fail only after deployment.
findings.correction: Service images install their explicit frozen extras, pin both base-image manifest digests, expose provider-specific KMS targets, and are built and scanned in CI.

findings.exploit: One aggregate critical-coverage percentage can conceal a subsystem below the required assurance threshold.
findings.correction: Coverage JSON is evaluated fail-closed for 12 explicitly named groups, including missing-file detection, after the unchanged 95 percent aggregate gate.

findings.exploit: Reference load and restore evidence can pass function tests but fail during serialization because floating-point timings violate CPCF canonical JSON.
findings.correction: All timing evidence is represented as integer microseconds and command-level harness execution is verified before CI admission.

findings.exploit: Strict type checking can pass while excluding operational release scripts that contain archive-scanner type defects.
findings.correction: CI and release mypy commands include scripts, and zip/tar archive member types use distinct variables.

findings.exploit: A trusted_time blocker begins with trust and can be routed to trust-policy repair instead of authoritative time evidence when namespace prefixes overlap.
findings.affected_claim: Typed and command-safe first-agent repair guidance
findings.correction: Time namespaces are evaluated before generic trust namespaces, and every registered repair prefix is checked against exact document-kind and authority outputs.

findings.exploit: A mutation score can fail without retaining the survivor list, forcing an expensive rerun and encouraging speculative test changes or pressure to weaken the gate.
findings.correction: CI and release workflows upload the complete Mutmut status list as a 14-day diagnostic artifact before enforcing the unchanged 85 percent score.

findings.exploit: A service image build omits wheel forced-includes and fails before vulnerability scanning, while unconditional SARIF upload obscures the primary failure with missing-file errors.
findings.affected_claim: Reproducible and scanned API/worker OCI artifacts
findings.correction: Both service build contexts include every wheel-forced agent, fixture, and documentation input, and SARIF upload is conditional on the scanner output existing.

findings.exploit: The no-checkout release-assets job cannot infer a repository from Git state, causing verified artifacts to remain unattached and preventing the dependent trusted-publishing job.
findings.affected_claim: Release artifact delivery and trusted publication
findings.correction: The release-assets job now binds GH_REPO to the immutable GitHub repository context instead of relying on an absent checkout.
